sub_problems: What is the right metric of 'size' for agent memory items (frequency-of-retrieval, embedding-cluster-centrality, semantic-richness)?
How does the coarsening rate depend on retrieval frequency, embedding-similarity proximity, and consolidation cadence?
How does the system protect specialized small items from absorption when they are operationally important but rarely retrieved?
What is the equilibrium of the coarsening dynamic — does it converge to a few large items, or does new ingestion replenish small items continuously?
